# GROUNDED CONTEXT PACK — external-model design review

Source of truth: repo `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent` at STABLE baseline commit `78fe2ca`
(read via `git show 78fe2ca:<path>`; the working tree has an in-progress build — IGNORE it).
Proposal under review: `/home/polaris/polaris_project/GATE_FIX_PLAN_CONSOLIDATED.md` (Fix 4 + Fix 5).

All code below is VERBATIM from the baseline commit unless marked `... [elided] ...`.
Line numbers are baseline line numbers.

===== 0. ABSOLUTE RULES + OPERATOR STANCE (read first) =====

1. FAITHFULNESS IS FROZEN. Never touch `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py`,
   strict_verify, or the drop rule. No new verification pass, no new faithfulness test. A prior
   project ("Cellcog") died from fiddling with the verifier; the operator will not risk a sudden
   score collapse. Every fix below is explicitly designed to sit UPSTREAM of the frozen verifier
   (it changes which rows are ELIGIBLE to cite, never HOW a claim is verified) or to be RENDER-ONLY
   (§-1.3: placement/repetition is QUALITY not faithfulness), and to be OFF-path byte-identical.
2. ENFORCE SCOPE AT RETRIEVAL, NEVER FILTER A FROZEN CORPUS. Filtering the 997-row unscoped corpus
   down to 131 journal rows tanked RACE 0.4447 -> 0.3264 (even Instruction-Following FELL). Scope
   must make the retriever GO FIND in-scope sources / re-order a rich menu — never starve it.
   (This is the empirical reason Fix 5 makes journal-only a PREFERENCE + audit, not a hard mask.)

OPERATOR OVER-ENGINEERING AVERSION: prefer the smallest faithfulness-safe change that captures the
gain; avoid new subsystems, new flags-on-by-default, or clever rewrites of verified text. The plan's
own verdict is that the gate's job THIS run is "prove it is not a regression" (reach ~parity with the
champion's IF 0.4587), not to beat the champion. Kill-switches default to prior behaviour.

===== 1a. FIX 4 (VERBATIM from GATE_FIX_PLAN_CONSOLIDATED.md) =====

### FIX 4 — Report shape: ship a literature review, not an audit dump (direct IF fix) — Effort M/L
Four VERIFIED mechanisms behind IF −0.091 / Readability −0.150 (all in NON-frozen render assembly):
1. **No H1, opens on a blockquote** (verified: report.md line 1 = `> **STRONGEST VERIFIER…`; champion opens `# A literature review on…`).
2. **Machinery-first** — Key Findings + audit blocks precede any thematic section.
3. **Instruction visibly violated + unevaluated** (fixed by Fix 2 + Fix 5).
4. **Malformed bullet** — VERIFIED report.md line 9 = `Job Displacement by AI Technologies.** …`; the opening `**` fragment was chopped at the render seam.

Changes:
- **(a) Title + claim-free intro.** At the sweep report-assembly seam, emit `# {title}` (title already exists) + a short `## Introduction and Scope` framing paragraph from the contract objective. Faithfulness-safe: asserts no findings, contains no citations, identical class to the champion's shipped intro.
- **(b) Demote machinery below the appendix boundary** (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:6325`). On-disk order: Title → Intro → thematic `###` → Synthesis → Limitations → Bibliography → `## Appendix (not scored as report claims)` containing methods/disclosure/contradiction ledgers/reliability. **Position only — nothing deleted; disclosure stays in the shipped file AND manifest.** Per CLAUDE.md §-1.3 placement/repetition is QUALITY not faithfulness (precedent: render-only dedup at `:20802`).
- **(c) Key-Findings integrity invariant** in `src/polaris_graph/generator/key_findings.py` bullet emission: every bullet must open with `**` and contain a matched closing `**`; a failing bullet is re-emitted from its stored title + carried sentence (both exist verbatim upstream), never shipped chopped. Constrain the render-seam chrome removal to whole-unit granularity inside the Key Findings block.
- **(d)** Shrink the hedge preamble (report.md line 7) to one sentence + appendix pointer (`key_findings.py` preamble ~:1004).
- **Faithfulness:** every moved block is disclosure/audit text; every kept finding sentence is byte-identical strict_verify output; frozen files 0-diff. Bullet re-emission uses only verbatim verified text.
- **RACE impact:** +0.03–0.06 IF, +0.04–0.07 Readability, +0.01–0.03 Comprehensiveness.

===== 1b. FIX 5 (VERBATIM from GATE_FIX_PLAN_CONSOLIDATED.md) =====

### FIX 5 — Tame the (about-to-arm) mask + fix the journal leak, WITHOUT touching strict_verify — Effort M — **ship WITH Fix 2**
Once Fix 2 lands, `build_quality_eligibility` arms against a 41%-UNKNOWN corpus; the hard branch would mask FAIL+UNKNOWN → 48% → worse than today. Pair these:
- **(a) Resolve UNKNOWN, don't mask it:** `PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING=1` (it was OFF; zero tiering lines in log). Collapses the 41% UNKNOWN to real tiers before the mask reads them. Metadata enrichment, faithfulness-neutral.
- **(b) Deterministic second-chance before UNKNOWN** in `quality_eligibility.py:score_source_quality`, insert before the UNKNOWN fail-closed at :222/:224: if the row has a DOI or classifies as a journal article (`document_type_classifier.is_peer_reviewed_journal_article` — the same predicate the scope classifier already trusts), return PASS. **Evidence-POSITIVE only** — the FAIL verdicts (retracted, predatory, `is_peer_reviewed=False` at :209, low tier) are UNTOUCHED. Extend `_PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS` with `abacademies.org` (bib [9], predatory-adjacent, shipped).
- **(c) Two-tier topicality (this run's ACTUAL over-mask).** VERIFIED: 50 exclusions, **38 in the 0.15–0.30 band** (on-topic-adjacent), only 12 truly off-topic (<0.15). At the call site `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:14369-14371`: introduce `PG_TOPICALITY_HARD_FLOOR` (default 0.15) for HARD quarantine; pass the 0.15–0.30 band through the existing SOFT-demote path (`quality_eligibility.py:350`). Confirmed junk (<0.15) still hard-quarantines.
- **(d) Journal-only = visible preference + measured compliance, NOT a starvation mask.** With Fix 2, `allowed_source_kinds=['peer_reviewed_journal']` (hard) becomes an `op=prefer` facet (`retrieval_projection.py:314`). Enforcement, in safety order: (1) selection ordering — journal/DOI rows first in the selected menu (bibliography share rises, nothing dropped); (2) S4 audit now scores real journal share instead of UNKNOWN; (3) HARD journal-only (`PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY=1`) ONLY behind a corpus-adequacy pre-check (≥~25 T1/T2/DOI rows post-tiering), else disclose "insufficient journal corpus; prioritized journals" rather than starve. **Empirical justification: the champion scored IF 0.4587 while itself citing majority non-journal** — a hard mask on this 9-journal corpus would crater Comprehensiveness.
- **Faithfulness:** all changes shape the citable MENU upstream of the frozen verifier; no FAIL row re-admitted; UNKNOWN handling adds evidence, not leniency; provenance_generator/strict_verify 0-diff.
- **RACE impact:** +0.02–0.04 Comp/Insight (menu 93 → ~120–135 of 143), +0.01–0.03 IF (visible journal compliance + audit SATISFIED rows).

===== 2a. CONTRACT DATA MODEL — deontic-strength enum + generic-IR vocabulary (planning_gate_schema.py) =====

# NOTE ON NAMING: the plan says "deliverable_spec / scope_spec". In the ACTUAL baseline the
# ResearchContract holds these as FLAT `list[ContractTerm]` keyed by a free-string `dimension`
# (e.g. "scope.source_types", "deliverable.kind"). There is no separate DeliverableSpec class.
# The "deontic-strength enum" is the FORCE_* constants below (hard | preference | open); a term's
# strength lives in `ContractTerm.force`, and a DeonticCue (clause_ledger.py) carries the cue's force.

# --- FORCE (the deontic-strength enum) — planning_gate_schema.py:94 ---
FORCE_HARD = "hard"            # binding inclusion / exclusion / shape requirement
FORCE_PREFER = "preference"    # optimize when feasible; may NEVER starve coverage
FORCE_OPEN = "open"            # intentionally unspecified
FORCES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({FORCE_HARD, FORCE_PREFER, FORCE_OPEN})

STAGES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"retrieval","outline","compose","render","audit"})

# --- generic relation a term asserts (Phase B constraint-IR) — :121 ---
OP_IN = "IN"            # membership: attribute ∈ value_set   (allowed-kind set)
OP_NOT_IN = "NOT_IN"    # exclusion:  attribute ∉ value_set   ("no blogs")
OP_EQ = "EQ"            # equality:   attribute == value
OP_GTE = "GTE"          # lower bound: attribute >= value      (from 2024 onward)
OP_LTE = "LTE"          # upper bound: attribute <= value      (before 2020)
OP_BETWEEN = "BETWEEN"  # interval:   value <= attribute <= value2
OP_REQUIRE = "REQUIRE"  # obligation: attribute must be covered/present
OP_PREFER = "PREFER"    # soft optimize: attribute preferred, never gates
OPERATORS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({OP_IN,OP_NOT_IN,OP_EQ,OP_GTE,OP_LTE,OP_BETWEEN,OP_REQUIRE,OP_PREFER})

STAGE_OWNERS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"retrieval","ranking","eligibility","compose","render"})

# normalization status: exact (registry-mapped) | proposed (LLM, unconfirmed) | opaque (preserved
# verbatim, cannot yet enforce). An opaque HARD term is lossless-preserved + surfaced as
# blocked_unsupported — never silently dropped. — :149
NORM_EXACT = "exact"; NORM_PROPOSED = "proposed"; NORM_OPAQUE = "opaque"

# --- canonical SOURCE-scope dimension keys (what the retrieval projection reads) — :168 ---
SCOPE_SOURCE_TYPES = "scope.source_types"          # e.g. journal_article
SCOPE_SOURCE_QUALITY = "scope.source_quality"      # e.g. high (high-quality/peer-reviewed)
SCOPE_SOURCE_LANGUAGES = "scope.source_languages"  # SOURCE language, e.g. en / English
SCOPE_EXCLUDED_SOURCE_KINDS = "scope.excluded_source_kinds"  # "no blogs" -> op=exclude facet, NEVER query text
SCOPE_OPAQUE = "scope.opaque"       # deontic clause no parser normalized; post-fetch eligibility judged
CONTENT_OPAQUE = "content.opaque"   # topic obligation; a compose-side note
SCHEMA_VERSION = "planning-gate/1.0"

# --- ContractTerm (the tagged unit; strength = .force) — :391 ---
@dataclass
class ContractTerm:
    term_id: str
    dimension: str
    value: Any = None
    origin: str = ORIGIN_INFERRED
    force: str = FORCE_OPEN            # <-- deontic strength: hard | preference | open
    confidence: float = 0.0
    spans: list[PromptSpan] = field(default_factory=list)
    rationale: str = ""
    policy_id: Optional[str] = None
    enforcement_stages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    editable: bool = True
    # generic constraint-IR fields (Phase B; all OPTIONAL / default-empty; to_dict skips at default
    # so an old term hashes byte-identically — the OFF-path guardrail):
    subject: str = ""                    # e.g. source, evidence, output, section
    attribute: str = ""                  # e.g. kind, published_at, language, quality
    operator: str = ""                   # one of OPERATORS ("" == unset / legacy)
    value_set: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)  # for IN / NOT_IN
    boolean_group: str = ""
    stage_owner: str = ""                # one of STAGE_OWNERS
    capability_id: str = ""
    normalization_status: str = ""       # exact | proposed | opaque

    def is_hard(self) -> bool: return self.force == FORCE_HARD
    def is_opaque(self) -> bool: return self.normalization_status == NORM_OPAQUE

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        out = {"term_id":self.term_id,"dimension":self.dimension,"value":self.value,
               "origin":self.origin,"force":self.force,"confidence":self.confidence,
               "spans":[s.to_dict() for s in self.spans],"rationale":self.rationale,
               "policy_id":self.policy_id,"enforcement_stages":list(self.enforcement_stages),
               "editable":self.editable}
        # emit each generic-IR field ONLY when set (non-default) -> legacy shape hashes byte-identical
        if self.subject: out["subject"] = self.subject
        if self.attribute: out["attribute"] = self.attribute
        if self.operator: out["operator"] = self.operator
        if self.value_set: out["value_set"] = list(self.value_set)
        if self.boolean_group: out["boolean_group"] = self.boolean_group
        if self.stage_owner: out["stage_owner"] = self.stage_owner
        if self.capability_id: out["capability_id"] = self.capability_id
        if self.normalization_status: out["normalization_status"] = self.normalization_status
        return out
    # from_dict: reads generic-IR fields if present; an out-of-vocab operator/stage_owner is kept
    # verbatim (NOT coerced) so validate_contract surfaces it as a bad-enum error. ... [elided] ...

===== 2b. ResearchContract (the typed contract; scope/deliverable are ContractTerm lists) — planning_gate_schema.py:746 =====

@dataclass
class ResearchContract:
    """Grouped as objective / scope / content / deliverable, held as flat ContractTerm lists
    keyed by `dimension` (objective.question, scope.source_types, scope.date, deliverable.kind)
    plus the two structured collections (coverage, sections)."""
    schema_version: str = SCHEMA_VERSION
    objective: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
    scope: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
    content_terms: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
    deliverable: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)   # <-- the "deliverable_spec"
    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = field(default_factory=list)
    sections: list[SectionRequirement] = field(default_factory=list)
    ambiguities: list[Ambiguity] = field(default_factory=list)
    assumptions: list[Assumption] = field(default_factory=list)      # disclosure ledger (inferred/policy-default origins)
    conflicts: list[ContractConflict] = field(default_factory=list)
    complexity: str = "standard"
    compiler_degraded: bool = False

    def all_terms(self) -> list[ContractTerm]:
        terms = []
        terms.extend(self.objective); terms.extend(self.scope)
        terms.extend(self.content_terms); terms.extend(self.deliverable)
        for cr in self.coverage: terms.append(cr.statement); terms.extend(cr.compare_on)
        for sec in self.sections: terms.append(sec.title)
        return terms
    def hard_terms(self) -> list[ContractTerm]: return [t for t in self.all_terms() if t.is_hard()]

    # S2 retrieval-projection compile methods (thin wrappers over retrieval_projection):
    def to_research_frame(self, plan=None): ...  # route scope onto champion ResearchFrame (None => champion path)
    def to_scope_terms(self, plan=None) -> dict[str, list[str]]: ...  # hard/soft split + language lanes + evidence needs

    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        return {"schema_version":self.schema_version,
                "objective":[t.to_dict() for t in self.objective],
                "scope":[t.to_dict() for t in self.scope],
                "content_terms":[t.to_dict() for t in self.content_terms],
                "deliverable":[t.to_dict() for t in self.deliverable],
                "coverage":[c.to_dict() for c in self.coverage],
                "sections":[s.to_dict() for s in self.sections],
                "ambiguities":[a.to_dict() for a in self.ambiguities],
                "assumptions":[a.to_dict() for a in self.assumptions],
                "conflicts":[c.to_dict() for c in self.conflicts],
                "complexity":self.complexity,"compiler_degraded":self.compiler_degraded}

===== 2c. RetrievalPolicy — allowed_source_kinds + the contract->enforcer projection (retrieval_projection.py:185, :314) =====

@dataclass
class RetrievalPolicy:
    """Typed, hash-stamped policy compiled from the pinned contract + plan. The DECLARATIVE
    predicate set a caller executes as backend filters, prefetch ranking, and a post-fetch
    citable-eligibility verdict — ALWAYS upstream of the frozen verifier (changes which rows are
    ELIGIBLE to cite, never HOW a claim is verified). Pure data: no network, no LLM, no I/O."""
    allowed_source_kinds: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # hard scope.source_types values; kind matching NONE (when non-empty) is out of the citable menu
    excluded_source_kinds: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # NEGATIVE predicate; matching ANY is masked; NEVER positive query text
    date_from: Optional[str] = None
    date_to: Optional[str] = None
    languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)              # hard scope.source_languages
    named_inclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    named_exclusions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    quality_profile: Optional[str] = None                          # REF to a domain-neutral quality profile (e.g. "high"); never a query word
    opaque_eligibility: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)     # F2: hard un-normalized deontic clauses, judged post-fetch; NEVER a query string
    predicate_force: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)   # {predicate_key: "hard"|"soft"} — which are gating vs ranking-only
    contract_hash: str = ""

    def is_empty(self) -> bool:  # permissive/empty-contract case => caller applies NO filter (byte-identical)
        return not (self.allowed_source_kinds or self.excluded_source_kinds or self.date_from
                    or self.date_to or self.languages or self.named_inclusions
                    or self.named_exclusions or self.quality_profile or self.opaque_eligibility)
    def to_dict(self): ...  # straight field dump  [elided]

    # -- the contract -> protocol BRIDGE: compiles into the LEGACY `protocol` shape the FROZEN
    #    constraint_enforcement.build_scope_enforcement reads. allowed_source_kinds -> op=prefer
    #    facets; excluded_source_kinds -> op=exclude facets. Strictness=hard iff predicate marked hard.
    #    A value resolving to no facet is emitted as opaque:<value> (NEVER silently dropped).
    def to_scope_protocol(self, *, ontology=None) -> dict[str, Any]:
        facets: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        seen_facets: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
        def _facet(facet_id, dimension, op, hard):
            key = (facet_id, op)
            if not facet_id or key in seen_facets: return
            seen_facets.add(key)
            facets.append({"facet_id":facet_id,"dimension":dimension,"op":op,
                           "strictness":"hard" if hard else "weight","trigger_span":""})
        ont = ontology if ontology is not None else _load_scope_ontology_safe()
        allowed_hard = str((self.predicate_force or {}).get("allowed_source_kinds","soft")).lower() == "hard"
        excluded_hard = str((self.predicate_force or {}).get("excluded_source_kinds","soft")).lower() == "hard"
        # ***** :314 — how allowed_source_kinds is projected today (the Fix 5d anchor) *****
        for kind in self.allowed_source_kinds:
            fid, dim = _resolve_facet_id(kind, ont)
            _facet(fid, dim, "prefer", allowed_hard)   # <-- ALREADY "prefer"; hardness gated by predicate_force
        for kind in self.excluded_source_kinds:
            fid, dim = _resolve_facet_id(kind, ont)
            _facet(fid, dim, "exclude", excluded_hard)
        # named in/exclusions -> enforcer named_include/named_exclude (named-exclude always hard)
        # ... [elided: named lists, date_range, timeline/language/quality hard wires] ...

# KEY OBSERVATION for the reviewer: at :314 an allowed_source_kind is ALREADY emitted as op="prefer".
# The `strictness` is "hard" ONLY when predicate_force["allowed_source_kinds"]=="hard". So Fix 5d's
# "becomes an op=prefer facet" is describing keeping strictness=weight (not setting the hard wire),
# i.e. the change is in what predicate_force carries + the selection-ordering/audit layers, NOT a new
# projection branch.

===== 2d. Clause ledger — DeonticCue + force inheritance (clause_ledger.py) =====

# FORCE_HARD / FORCE_PREFER imported from planning_gate_schema. A clause's deontic cue sets its force.
# families: exclude/restrict/oblige => FORCE_HARD ; date/quality/prefer => FORCE_PREFER. — :240-261
@dataclass
class DeonticCue:
    """The deontic cue that made a clause constraint-bearing."""
    cue: str
    family: str        # exclude | restrict | oblige | date | quality | prefer
    force: str         # FORCE_HARD or FORCE_PREFER
    attribute: str     # kind | coverage | published_at | quality | ""

# _deontic_hit(clause_text) returns the most-specific cue; e.g.
#   exclude/restrict -> force=FORCE_HARD, attribute="kind"
#   oblige           -> force=FORCE_HARD, attribute="coverage"
#   date             -> force=FORCE_PREFER, attribute="published_at"
#   quality          -> force=FORCE_PREFER, attribute="quality"
# CandidateConstraint carries `.deontic`; is_constraint => deontic is not None. — :364
# hard_restriction_spans(): verbatim spans of every clause whose cue family in
#   (restrict, exclude, oblige) AND force == FORCE_HARD. — :826
# author_opaque_terms(): a deontic clause NO term covered becomes a scope.opaque / content.opaque
#   ContractTerm, force inherited from its cue, raw clause text + span preserved. — :878

===== 3. quality_eligibility.py (VERBATIM — the Fix 5b/5c surface) =====

PASS = "pass"; FAIL = "fail"; UNKNOWN = "unknown"

# --- predatory host patterns (Fix 5b extends this tuple with "abacademies.org") — :142 ---
_PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "/ojs/",           # bare Open Journal Systems mill path
    "scirp.org",       # Scientific Research Publishing (widely-flagged mill)
    "m.scirp.org",
    "sciencepublishinggroup.com",
    "omicsonline.org",
    "omicsgroup.org",
    "hindawi-",        # spoofed-hindawi mills (real hindawi.com is NOT matched)
)
_HIGH_QUALITY_TOKENS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
    "high","high-quality","high quality","peer-reviewed","peer reviewed","peer_reviewed",
    "reputable","authoritative","scholarly"})
def _is_high_quality_request(quality_profile): 
    q = str(quality_profile or "").strip().lower(); return bool(q) and q in _HIGH_QUALITY_TOKENS
def _predatory_host(url):
    u = (url or "").lower(); host = ""
    try: host = (urlparse(u).netloc or "").lower()
    except Exception: host = ""
    for pat in _PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS:
        if pat in u or pat in host: return True
    return False

# --- score_source_quality: the verdict function; Fix 5b inserts a DOI/journal PASS *before* the
#     UNKNOWN fail-closed returns at :222 and :226. FAIL branches (5b says) stay UNTOUCHED. — :177 ---
def score_source_quality(row) -> tuple[str, float, str]:
    """Returns (verdict in {PASS,FAIL,UNKNOWN}, weight in (0,1], basis).
    PASS = positively-evidenced high quality; FAIL = positively-evidenced low quality
    (retracted / predatory host / low tier / non-peer-reviewed shell); UNKNOWN = no usable quality
    metadata (fail-CLOSED at the hard seam). weight = the SOFT-preference demote (never 0)."""
    # 1) retraction — always disqualifying, highest precedence.
    if bool(_row_get(row, "is_retracted", False)):
        return FAIL, 0.05, "retracted (is_retracted=True)"
    url = _row_url(row)
    # 2) predatory / OJS-mill host — journal-shaped but not peer-reviewed.
    if _predatory_host(url):
        return FAIL, 0.1, f"predatory/OJS-mill host heuristic matched ({url})"
    tier = str(_row_get(row, "tier", "") or "").strip().upper()
    pr = _row_get(row, "is_peer_reviewed", None)  # None = not fetched (absent)
    # 3) explicit peer-review evidence.
    if pr is True and tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
        return PASS, 1.0, f"peer_reviewed=True AND tier={tier} (high-quality band)"
    if pr is False:   # journal-SHAPED but explicitly NOT peer-reviewed -> FAIL  (:209)
        return FAIL, 0.2, f"is_peer_reviewed=False (journal-shaped != peer-reviewed); tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}"
    # 4) tier-only evidence (peer-review flag absent).
    if tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
        return PASS, 1.0, f"tier={tier} (high-quality band; peer_reviewed flag absent)"
    if tier in _LOW_QUALITY_TIERS:
        return FAIL, 0.3, f"tier={tier} (low-quality band: industry/news/blog/stub)"
    # 5) content-shell degradation -> UNKNOWN (fail-closed under hard) with a demote.  (:222)
    if (bool(_row_get(row,"content_starved",False)) or bool(_row_get(row,"landing_page",False))
            or bool(_row_get(row,"citation_metadata_shell",False))):
        return UNKNOWN, 0.4, "content shell/starved (no quality metadata; fail-closed under hard)"
    # 6) no usable quality metadata -> UNKNOWN (fail-CLOSED under hard; soft keeps neutral weight). (:226)
    return UNKNOWN, 0.6, f"no quality metadata (tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}, peer_reviewed absent) — fail-closed under hard"

# --- build_quality_eligibility: applies the verdict; HARD masks FAIL+UNKNOWN (the fail-closed path
#     Fix 5 warns "arms against a 41%-UNKNOWN corpus"); SOFT only demotes. — :234 ---
def build_quality_eligibility(policy, evidence_rows, *, quality_term_id="scope.source_quality") -> EligibilityPlan:
    plan = EligibilityPlan()
    quality_profile = getattr(policy, "quality_profile", None)
    if not _is_high_quality_request(quality_profile):
        return plan  # no hard/soft quality predicate -> byte-identical no-op
    force = (getattr(policy,"predicate_force",{}) or {}).get("quality_profile","soft")
    is_hard = str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
    contract_hash = str(getattr(policy,"contract_hash","") or "")
    for row in list(evidence_rows or []):
        url = _row_url(row)
        if not url: continue
        verdict, weight, basis = score_source_quality(row)
        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=quality_term_id,
            source_id=url, stage=STAGE_QUALITY, verdict=verdict, basis=basis))
        if is_hard:
            # HARD: FAIL and UNKNOWN are both out of the citable menu (fail-closed).  <<-- the over-mask
            if verdict in (FAIL, UNKNOWN):
                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
                plan.excluded_records.append({"source_id":url,"stage":STAGE_QUALITY,
                    "verdict":verdict,"basis":basis,"force":"hard"})
        else:
            # SOFT: order-not-drop. A FAIL/UNKNOWN demotes; never excluded.
            if weight < 1.0: plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = weight
    return plan

# --- build_topicality_eligibility: the SOFT-demote path Fix 5c routes the 0.15-0.30 band through
#     (see the `else: plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = max(0.05, score)` at :351). — :289 ---
def build_topicality_eligibility(evidence_rows, *, objective, thread_queries=None, floor,
        scorer, contract_hash="", topicality_term_id="topicality", is_hard=True) -> EligibilityPlan:
    """Post-fetch topical eligibility vs the CLEAN objective + owning thread. Reuses the SAME
    embedding-cosine scorer live retrieval uses pre-fetch. Confirmed off-topic (score < floor) is
    quarantined (HARD) / down-ranked (SOFT); uncertain (>= floor) stays. None score => UNKNOWN,
    fail-OPEN (never quarantine a source we could not score — topicality is not a user prohibition)."""
    plan = EligibilityPlan()
    rows = list(evidence_rows or [])
    if not rows or not (objective or "").strip(): return plan
    row_dicts = [{"statement": _row_text(r), "direct_quote": ""} for r in rows]
    try: score_map = scorer(objective, list(thread_queries or []), row_dicts)
    except Exception: score_map = None
    if score_map is None:
        # embedder unavailable -> UNKNOWN, fail-OPEN (no quarantine); receipts only.  [elided loop]
        return plan
    for idx, r in enumerate(rows):
        url = _row_url(r)
        if not url: continue
        score = float(score_map.get(idx, 0.0))
        if score < floor:
            verdict = FAIL
            basis = f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} < floor {floor:.3f} (confirmed off-topic)"
            if is_hard:
                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
                plan.excluded_records.append({"source_id":url,"stage":STAGE_TOPICALITY,
                    "verdict":verdict,"basis":basis,"force":"hard"})
            else:
                plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = max(0.05, score)   # <-- :351 SOFT-demote (Fix 5c band)
        else:
            verdict = PASS; basis = f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} >= floor {floor:.3f}"
        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=topicality_term_id,
            source_id=url, stage=STAGE_TOPICALITY, verdict=verdict, basis=basis))
    return plan

===== 4. Report-assembly seam — scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py (the Fix 4 surfaces) =====

# --- 4.1 assemble_report_md — the final report.md assembler (:5230) ---
def assemble_report_md(title_md, abstract_md, body_md, conclusion_md, *, dedup_enabled) -> str:
    """Abstract(front)+Conclusion(end) are intentional verbatim re-presentations of body sentences,
    EXEMPT from paragraph-dedup. When a summary renders, dedup BODY ONLY + sandwich abstract/conclusion.
    When NO summary (abstract==conclusion==""), use the EXACT pre-PR-d path -- dedup(title+body) over
    the whole thing -- so default-OFF render is BYTE-IDENTICAL. Pure."""
    if abstract_md or conclusion_md:
        body = dedup_identical_paragraphs(body_md) if dedup_enabled else body_md
        return title_md + abstract_md + body + conclusion_md
    whole = title_md + body_md
    return dedup_identical_paragraphs(whole) if dedup_enabled else whole

# --- 4.2 the SUCCESS-PATH title + body-order concat + assemble call (:17254) ---
#     THIS is the "report-assembly seam" Fix 4(a) (title/intro) and 4(b) (machinery order) target.
        _assembled_title = (
            f"# Research report: {_strip_injected_instruction_appendix(q['question'])}\n\n"
        )
        # ... [elided: _cwf_disclosed_md build — a kept-but-not-cited disclosure block, default-ON] ...
        _assembled_body = (
            _key_findings + sections_concat + _depth_layer + methods
            + biblio_section + _cwf_disclosed_md + _drop_disclosure_md
        )   # <-- CURRENT on-disk ORDER: Key Findings FIRST, then thematic sections, depth, methods,
            #     bibliography, disclosures. Fix 4(b) reorders: Title->Intro->thematic->synthesis->
            #     limitations->Bibliography->Appendix(machinery). Note there is NO "# {title}" +
            #     "## Introduction and Scope" today, and _key_findings leads the body (machinery-first).
        try:
            final_report = assemble_report_md(_assembled_title, _abstract_md, _assembled_body,
                _conclusion_md, dedup_enabled=_env_flag(_LIMITATIONS_DEDUP_ENV, default=True))
        except Exception as _assemble_exc:  # finish-line: a rendered report MUST ship (fail-open concat)
            final_report = (_assembled_title + (f"{_abstract_md}\n\n" if _abstract_md else "")
                + _assembled_body + ...)   # [elided]

# --- 4.3 the AUDIT-MACHINERY APPENDIX boundary (:6313-6371) — Fix 4(b) references :6325 ---
_AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_ENV = "PG_AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX"
_AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY = (
    "## Appendix: audit, disclosure, and weighting (not scored as report claims)"   # :6325
)
def _audit_machinery_appendix_enabled() -> bool:  # default ON; OFF => machinery prepended (pre-fix)
    return os.environ.get(_AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_ENV,"1").strip().lower() not in ("","0","false","no","off")

def compose_report_with_reliability(final_report, reliability_md) -> str:
    """Place the reliability/audit MACHINERY as a trailing typed APPENDIX (default) instead of a body
    PREPEND. Guarantees: scored body FIRST; machinery preserved verbatim under one typed boundary;
    nothing dropped. OFF (or empty machinery) => legacy prepend, byte-identical."""
    rel = (reliability_md or "").strip(); body = final_report or ""
    if not rel: return body
    from src.polaris_graph.generator.citation_layer_policy import is_audit_appendix_block as _is_audit_appendix_block
    _to_appendix = _audit_machinery_appendix_enabled() and _is_audit_appendix_block("reliability_header")
    if not _to_appendix:
        return reliability_md + body  # legacy prepend (pre-fix byte-identical)
    return body.rstrip() + "\n\n" + _AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY + "\n\n" + rel + "\n"
# PRECEDENT the plan cites: this appendix relocation + the :20802 render-only dedup are already-
# shipped, faithfulness-neutral render-structure moves. Fix 4(b) extends the SAME pattern.

# --- 4.4 the D8 BANNER prepend site (:20778) — Fix 4 item 1 "opens on a blockquote" ---
#     The banner FUNCTION lives in the FROZEN provenance_generator.py:3212 (do NOT edit it — the plan
#     REJECTS PG_REPORT_D8_BANNER=0; the honest removal is Fix 3 making D8 actually adjudicate).
        if _env_flag("PG_REPORT_D8_BANNER", default=True):
            try:
                from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import build_d8_unadjudicated_banner
                _d8_banner = build_d8_unadjudicated_banner(manifest.get("release_disclosure"))
                if _d8_banner:
                    _banner_report_path = run_dir / "report.md"
                    if _banner_report_path.is_file():
                        _banner_existing = _banner_report_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                        if "STRONGEST VERIFIER (four-role D8) DID NOT RUN" not in _banner_existing:  # idempotency marker
                            _banner_report_path.write_text(_d8_banner + _banner_existing, encoding="utf-8")  # <-- PREPEND
            except Exception as _db_exc:  # additive disclosure; never abort the run
                _log(f"[d8-banner]   skipped (fail-open): {_db_exc}")

# --- 4.5 the FROZEN banner builder (provenance_generator.py:3212 — DO NOT EDIT; shown for context) ---
def build_d8_unadjudicated_banner(release_disclosure) -> str:
    """Emits the banner ONLY when release_disclosure is a dict with adjudicated == False (strict
    identity: missing/malformed/truthy => ""). Pure disclosure plumbing; asserts NO finding."""
    if not isinstance(release_disclosure, dict): return ""
    if release_disclosure.get("adjudicated") is not False: return ""
    return ("> **STRONGEST VERIFIER (four-role D8) DID NOT RUN for this run — findings are "
            "UNVERIFIED-by-D8.**\n>\n> The four-role D8 adjudication (the strongest faithfulness "
            "verifier) did not bind for this run, so the findings below carry only the strict_verify "
            "/ span-grounding / NLI evidence — NOT the final D8 adjudication. ... [elided] ...\n\n")

# --- 4.6 the abort-path titles (the ONLY places today that already emit "# Research report: {q}") ---
#     build_no_verified_sections_abort_body(:5862) and build_excessive_gap_abort_body(:5898) both
#     open with  f"# Research report: {_strip_injected_instruction_appendix(research_question)}\n\n".
#     So an H1 title already exists in the codebase for the abort path; Fix 4(a) brings the same H1
#     (+ a claim-free "## Introduction and Scope") to the SUCCESS path at :17254.

===== 5. key_findings.py — bullet emission + preamble (the Fix 4c/4d surface) =====

# build_key_findings: the "## Key Findings" block. Each bullet = first verified sentence (verbatim,
# citation intact) from each non-dropped section. Verified-only + extractive — never a new claim. — :877
def build_key_findings(sections, *, sentence_relevance=None) -> str:
    if not key_findings_enabled(): return ""
    candidates: list[tuple[float, str]] = []
    for sr in sections or []:
        if getattr(sr,"dropped_due_to_failure",False): continue
        if getattr(sr,"is_gap_stub",False) or getattr(sr,"sentences_verified",1) == 0: continue  # skip gap disclosures
        verified_text = _strip_leading_markdown_headers(getattr(sr,"verified_text","") or "")  # I-perm-008: strip leaked "### " header
        if not verified_text.strip(): continue
        title = humanize_section_title(getattr(sr,"title","") or "")
        _marker_cap = _max_key_findings_markers()
        for sentence in _first_verified_sentences(verified_text, _SENTENCES_PER_SECTION,
                sentence_relevance=sentence_relevance, demote_unanchored=True):
            weight = (_relevance_weight(sentence_relevance, sentence) if sentence_relevance is not None else 0.0)
            sentence = cap_citation_marker_runs(sentence, _marker_cap)   # cap each adjacent citation RUN (render-only)
            label = f"**{title}.** " if title else ""                    # <-- the "**Title.**" wrapper Fix 4c guards
            candidates.append((weight, f"- {label}{sentence}"))          # <-- BULLET EMISSION (:925)
    if sentence_relevance is not None:
        candidates = sorted(candidates, key=lambda c: -c[0])   # stable descending-weight; ties keep doc order
    bullets = [bullet for _, bullet in candidates][:_MAX_BULLETS]
    if not bullets: return ""
    # HONEST self-cert PREAMBLE (Fix 4d shrinks this to one sentence + appendix pointer) — :936
    header = ("## Key Findings\n\n"
        "_Each finding below is verbatim text carried up from a cited body span; it passes strict_verify "
        "(span bounds + numeric match + ≥2 content-word grounding) but is single-origin unless marked "
        "corroborated, and span-grounding is NOT a peer-reviewed or on-topic guarantee. Citations are "
        "the body's._\n\n")
    return header + "\n".join(bullets) + "\n\n"

# refilter_key_findings_block(:815): a line-scoped post-redaction pass that DROPS KF bullets that
# became a redaction STUB after the four-role seam. Its comment (:843) confirms each KF bullet is a
# clean single line ("- **Section.** ...") so a line-scoped drop is exact. This is where a bullet can
# lose its opening "**" fragment at the render seam (the Fix 4 item-4 "chopped bullet"); Fix 4c makes
# the invariant "open with ** AND matched closing **, else re-emit from stored title + carried sentence".

# NOTE on the "~:1004" preamble pointer in the plan: line 1004 at baseline is inside
# build_depth_layer's docstring (the "### Single-source findings" TWO-TIER synthesis block, a
# SEPARATE block from Key Findings). The KF preamble Fix 4d actually shrinks is the header string at
# :936-942 above. build_depth_layer also emits HONEST provenance sub-labels (cross-source bullets are
# GENERATOR-PHRASED then re-grounded, NOT verbatim lifts) — relevant if Fix 4d touches synthesis text.

===== 6. retrieval_projection.py :314 (how allowed_source_kinds is projected today) =====

# (Full function in §2c above — repeated here as the item-6 focus.)  to_scope_protocol() :314:
        for kind in self.allowed_source_kinds:
            fid, dim = _resolve_facet_id(kind, ont)   # ontology synonym table (LAW VI: no new vocab)
            _facet(fid, dim, "prefer", allowed_hard)  # op ALWAYS "prefer"; strictness "hard" iff
                                                      # predicate_force["allowed_source_kinds"]=="hard"
# _resolve_facet_id: a value that resolves to NO facet is emitted as opaque:<value> (never dropped;
# it simply matches no source in classify_source_facets => fail-open demote, but stays disclosed).
# => Fix 5d's journal-only=preference is achievable by keeping strictness=weight here and doing the
#    real work in (1) selection ordering, (2) S4 journal-share audit, (3) a corpus-adequacy-gated
#    HARD flag — NO new projection branch, consistent with the operator's over-engineering aversion.

===== 7. THE TWO ABSOLUTE RULES + OPERATOR AVERSION (6 lines) =====

1. FAITHFULNESS IS FROZEN: never edit provenance_generator.py / strict_verify / the drop rule; add
   no new verification pass or faithfulness test (a prior project died from verifier-fiddling).
2. ENFORCE SCOPE AT RETRIEVAL, NOT BY FILTERING A FROZEN CORPUS: hard-filtering the 997-row corpus to
   131 journal rows tanked RACE 0.4447->0.3264 (IF fell too) — scope must make the retriever GO FIND
   in-scope sources / re-order a rich menu, never starve it (this is why Fix 5d = preference+audit).
Every fix here is upstream-of-verifier (menu eligibility) or render-only (§-1.3 placement/repetition
= QUALITY not faithfulness), each behind a default-prior kill-switch, OFF-path byte-identical.
OPERATOR OVER-ENGINEERING AVERSION: smallest faithfulness-safe change that captures the gain; no new
subsystems / clever rewrites of verified text; the run's job is prove-not-a-regression, not beat-champion.

If `_official_gov_host` / `_reputable_newsroom` / `_issuer_primary` cannot be sourced cheaply from the existing facet classifier, ship **only the T1-scholarly + the "kind ∈ allowed" gate seeded by the existing journal helper** for v1, and leave T2/T3 host predicates as a labelled TODO. The generalization that matters most is the *shape* (signal→tier→∈allowed), so that adding a kind later is data, not a new branch.
166	
167	### 3.3 EXACT edits (Fix 5) — verified against the code
168	
169	1. **`quality_eligibility.py:score_source_quality` (:177)** — add keyword-only params `allowed_kinds: frozenset[str] = frozenset()`, `excluded_kinds: frozenset[str] = frozenset()` (defaults empty ⇒ every existing caller byte-identical). Replace the running build's journal-only second-chance (`_has_doi_or_journal_credential` PASS at "4.5") with the `_positive_signal_tier` resolution rule, inserted **after step 4 (:214, the low-tier FAIL) and before step 5 (:216, the shell UNKNOWN)** — so every FAIL return (retracted :193, predatory :199, `is_peer_reviewed=False` :207, low-tier :214) is lexically above it and untouched. `_has_doi_or_journal_credential` is **reused** as the T1-scholarly predicate (single source of truth, no duplicate journal logic).
170	
171	2. **`quality_eligibility.py:build_quality_eligibility` (:234)** — thread `allowed_kinds=frozenset(policy.allowed_source_kinds)`, `excluded_kinds=frozenset(policy.excluded_source_kinds)` into the `score_source_quality` call. Receipts carry the new basis strings automatically.
172	
173	3. **`quality_eligibility.py:_PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS` (:142)** — **KEEP the running build's `"abacademies.org"` entry.** Kind-agnostic denylist; no change.
174	
175	4. **`PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING=1` (Fix 5a)** — enrichment only, feeds the existing `tier` field read at :201. Already kind-agnostic; keep.
176	
177	5. **Topicality two-tier (Fix 5c) at `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:14369` region** — **KEEP the running build's `PG_TOPICALITY_HARD_FLOOR=0.15` + soft-demote of the 0.15–0.30 band as-is.** Already fully kind-agnostic (embedding cosine vs objective). No change.
178	
179	6. **Selection ordering (Fix 5d) — GENERALIZE the running build.** The running build sorts on `_is_journal_lead` (`"journal" in str(_k).lower()` + `_jo_credential`). Replace with a **kind-match key**:
180	```python
181	_allowed = frozenset(str(k).lower() for k in (getattr(_gate_policy,"allowed_source_kinds",None) or []))
182	def _kind_match(_r) -> bool:
183	    return classified_kind(_r) in _allowed      # classified_kind from the SAME facet classifier
184	evidence_for_gen = sorted(evidence_for_gen, key=lambda _r: 0 if _kind_match(_r) else 1)  # stable
185	```
186	Preserve the running build's `assert len == _n_before` count-invariant and the log line (reword "journal" → "in-scope-kind"). When `_allowed` is empty ⇒ every row is `False` ⇒ stable no-op reorder (byte-identical selection).
187	
188	7. **S4 audit parametrization** — wherever S4 scores "journal share", parametrize the numerator by `policy.allowed_source_kinds` (in-scope-kind share), never the literal "journal". *(Anchor to be located in `run_honest_sweep_r3.py` at implementation — flagged unanchored, see §6.)*
189	
190	8. **`corpus_kind_adequacy` (INV-4) + hard-arming flag rename** — see §3.5.
191	
192	### 3.4 Invariants and how each is coded
193	
194	- **INV-1 never re-admit a strict_verify FAIL.** Structural: all Fix-5 code lives in *eligibility* (which rows may enter the citable menu), always upstream of the frozen verifier. Eligibility neither reads nor writes verify verdicts; a PASS-promoted row's claims still go through the untouched verifier. Enforced by C1's 0-diff check.
195	- **INV-2 never override a contract EXCLUSION.** Two layers: (i) in the tier rule, `kind in excluded_kinds` is never PASSed (the T1 clause is unconditional PASS, so a T1-host *excluded-kind* row — e.g. a `.gov`-hosted **blog** under exclude-blogs — must be handled: **T1 PASS is by tier, but the exclude facet masks it out of the menu before selection**, so it never cites; (ii) exclude facet at `retrieval_projection.py` (`op="exclude"`, hard iff NOT_IN term FORCE_HARD) operates regardless of quality verdict. **OPUS refinement of Fable:** Fable claimed the tier rule itself blocks excluded kinds; but the T1 clause is unconditional in the table. The real guarantee is the exclude facet mask upstream of the menu — so state that as the load-bearing INV-2 layer, not the tier rule. Test: a T1 `.gov`-hosted blog under exclude-blogs must not appear in the citable menu/bibliography.
196	- **INV-3 evidence-POSITIVE only (verdict monotonicity).** The inserted block contains ONLY `return PASS`; every FAIL return is lexically above and untouched; UNKNOWN fall-throughs remain the else-path. Property: `∀ row, contract: new_verdict ∈ {old_verdict} ∪ ({PASS} if old_verdict==UNKNOWN else {})`. The kind-match ordering assigns no weights/verdicts, so it cannot newly fail anything.
197	
198	### 3.5 Corpus-adequacy + disclosure fallback (ANY kind, not just journals)
199	
200	```python
201	# quality_eligibility.py (new, ~15 LOC — NOT a new module)
202	def corpus_kind_adequacy(rows, allowed_kinds, *, min_rows=25) -> tuple[bool, int]:
203	    n = sum(1 for r in rows
204	            if classified_kind(r) in allowed_kinds
205	            and score_source_quality(r, allowed_kinds=allowed_kinds)[0] != FAIL)
206	    return (n >= min_rows, n)
207	```
208	
209	At the sweep call-site the kind-restriction `is_hard` arms **only** as `term_is_hard AND env_flag(PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD) AND adequate`; otherwise force degrades to *prefer* and one disclosure line is appended to the machinery block (which Fix 4 places in the appendix):
210	
211	> "Scope note: the prompt restricts sources to {kinds}; only {n} in-scope sources were retrievable, below the {min_rows} adequacy floor — in-scope sources were prioritized rather than exclusively enforced."
212	
213	This generalizes 5d(3): the plan's "≥25 T1/T2/DOI rows" measures *scholarliness*; the general count measures *in-scope-kind-ness*. On a 200-wire-story news-memo corpus the journal-literal count would flunk adequacy (or arm on the wrong thing) while the general count correctly passes. Adequacy gates only the *eligibility hard mask*; retrieval-side "go find in-scope sources" (C2) always runs — that keeps the count honest, not a self-fulfilling starvation.

215	**Flag rename:** `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY` → `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD` (kind-agnostic; reads kinds from the policy). The journal flag has **not shipped** (running build never added it — only selection ordering) so rename with **no alias** (C3). Default OFF.

217	**Latent journal-centrism — FLAGGED, NOT fixed (C3), with an OPUS code correction:** Fable claims `_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS` "FAILs `tier ∈ {industry/news/blog/stub}` — kind words inside a quality table." **Code check: this is imprecise.** `_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS = {"T5","T6","T7"}` (`quality_eligibility.py:137`) keys on **tier IDs, not kind words**; "industry/news/blog/stub" appears only in the human-readable *basis string* at :214. So there is **no kind-literal in the mask to generalize away** — the tier→quality mapping is upstream (whatever assigns T5-T7). The real contradiction case ("high-quality only" + "cite blogs") is a genuine user contradiction that must surface in `ResearchContract.conflicts` (`planning_gate_schema.py:162`), not be silently resolved. And `build_quality_eligibility` only arms when the contract explicitly requests high quality (`_is_high_quality_request` gate at :... tokens :... ), so a blogs-wanted contract without a quality clause never enters this gate. **V1 leaves the tier band untouched; do NOT generalize the tier table this run.**
218	
219	### 3.6 Faithfulness-safety (Fix 5)
220	
221	- All Fix-5 changes shape the citable **menu** upstream of the frozen verifier; no FAIL row re-admitted (INV-3); UNKNOWN handling adds *evidence* (positive signal), not leniency; `provenance_generator.py` / strict_verify **0-diff**.
222	- Selection ordering is promote-by-sort only — no weight, no verdict, count-invariant (`assert len == _n_before`).
223	- **PG_GATE-OFF byte-identical:** `allowed_kinds`/`excluded_kinds` default empty ⇒ `score_source_quality` byte-identical; `corpus_kind_adequacy` never called without a policy; the kind-match sort is a stable no-op when `_allowed` is empty; `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD` default OFF; `RetrievalPolicy.is_empty()` (:230) ⇒ caller applies NO filter. Champion path (no contract) ⇒ all Fix-5 code no-ops.
224	
225	---
226	
227	## 4. Where the models disagreed + OPUS adjudication
228	
229	Only Fable returned, so "disagreement" = OPUS's code cross-check vs Fable's claims:
230	
231	1. **Fable said: add a compiler edit to set `predicate_force['allowed_source_kinds']` from the term force.** **OPUS: already in the baseline** (`retrieval_projection.py:674`). Drop this edit.
232	2. **Fable said: `_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS` has kind-words (`blog/news/industry`) inside the quality FAIL band that punish contract-wanted kinds.** **OPUS: imprecise** — the set is `{T5,T6,T7}` tier IDs; kind words are only in the basis string. Nothing to generalize; the conflict-ledger route is correct. Keep the *conclusion* (don't touch it), correct the *reason*.
233	3. **Fable's tier table calls `is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(row)`.** **OPUS: type mismatch** — that predicate takes a `DocumentType`. Reuse the running build's `_has_doi_or_journal_credential(row, url)` (which already classifies first) as the T1-scholarly predicate.
234	4. **Fable's INV-2 leans on the tier rule blocking excluded kinds.** **OPUS: the T1 clause is unconditional PASS** — the load-bearing guarantee is the upstream *exclude facet mask*, not the tier rule. Restated in §3.4.
235	5. **Fable gated the KF preamble shrink (Fix 4d) behind `PG_REPORT_SKELETON`.** **OPUS: the running build already ships 4c/4d kind-agnostically with their own guarding** — keep as-is; do not re-gate.
236	
237	No cross-model contradictions to adjudicate (Codex/Kimi produced nothing).
238	
239	---
240	
241	## 5. What the CURRENT running build must REPLACE / REFACTOR (for the hardening pass)
242	
243	The working tree is the plan-faithful, **journal-flavored** Fix 4/5. Unwind list:
244	
245	| Location | Running build (journal-flavored) | Generalize to |
246	|---|---|---|
247	| `quality_eligibility.py` `_has_doi_or_journal_credential` + "4.5 second-chance" PASS | DOI OR peer-reviewed-journal → PASS (journal-only) | **Reuse as the T1-scholarly row** inside `_positive_signal_tier`; add gov/news/PR tiers + `kind ∈ allowed_kinds` gate (§3.2). Thread `allowed_kinds`/`excluded_kinds` params. |
248	| `run_honest_sweep_r3.py` Fix 5d `_is_journal_lead` / `"journal" in str(_k).lower()` selection sort | journal/DOI rows sorted first | **`_kind_match(row) ∈ allowed_source_kinds`** stable sort (§3.3.6). Keep the count-invariant assert; reword log. |
249	| `run_honest_sweep_r3.py` Fix 4a hardcoded `## Introduction and Scope` framing | one fixed framing heading for all reports | **`build_framing_md(contract, archetype)` + archetype table** (§2.1); heading varies by `deliverable.kind`; memo emits none (BLUF). |
250	| KF block ordering: fixed (KF leads body) | machinery-demote only, KF position fixed | **`order_report_blocks` with `kf_position`** so memo/brief KF leads, review KF after framing, explainer KF tail. |
251	| S4 "journal share" audit | scores journal share | parametrize numerator by `allowed_source_kinds` (§3.3.7). |
252	| Env flag (if any journal-only flag was added) | — (running build added selection ordering, not a JOURNAL_ONLY flag) | add `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD` (kind-agnostic) + `PG_REPORT_SKELETON`; both default OFF. |
253	
254	**KEEP unchanged (already general):** Fix 4c `_bullet_marker_integrity_ok`/`_reemit_key_findings_bullet`; Fix 4d preamble shrink; Fix 5b `abacademies.org` denylist entry; Fix 5c topicality two-tier `PG_TOPICALITY_HARD_FLOOR`; Fix 5a `PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING`.
255	
256	---
257	
258	## 6. Metamorphic CROSS-PROMPT tests (same code, swap contract fixture)
259	
260	Shared fixture: one synthetic **60-row corpus** with known ground truth — 10 DOI journals, 12 `.gov` reports, 15 wire news, 8 issuer press releases, 8 analyst blogs, 7 metadata-less UNKNOWNs — plus contract fixtures. **Zero code differences between runs; the only variable is the swapped contract.**
261	
262	- **M1 `journal` (hard, systematic lit review):** journal/DOI UNKNOWNs → PASS (T1); menu sorts journal rows first; S4 reports journal share; adequacy n=10 < 25 ⇒ hard degrades to prefer + disclosure line present. Fix 4: archetype=`review`, H1 + "Introduction and Scope", KF after framing, machinery below :6325.
263	- **M2 `news+press_release` (hard, decision memo):** wire + issuer-PR UNKNOWNs → PASS (T2/T3-∈-allowed); journals get NO ordering boost beyond T1 quality (first-page menu majority in-scope kinds, NOT journal-first); S4 reports news+PR share; adequacy n=23 < 25 ⇒ degrade+disclose. Fix 4: archetype=`memo`, **KF leads (BLUF)**, no "Introduction and Scope" anywhere.
264	- **M3 `government` (hard, policy brief):** `.gov` UNKNOWNs → PASS (T1-gov) **without DOI**; menu gov-first; disclosure absent iff adequacy passes at fixture n=12. Fix 4: archetype=`brief`, "Executive Summary" framing, KF leads.
265	- **M4 `blogs allowed` (soft prefer, market scan, no quality clause):** `build_quality_eligibility` returns empty plan (the `_is_high_quality_request` gate — no high-quality request); blog rows in menu, sorted first; nothing masked. Fix 4: archetype=`comparison`, "Scope and Criteria" framing.
266	- **M5 `exclude blogs` (hard NOT_IN):** blog rows masked by the exclude facet; **a T1 `.gov`-hosted blog row must NOT reach the citable menu** (INV-2 via facet mask, not tier rule); receipts show exclusion basis.

Fix 4: archetype defaults `review` **with an Assumption ledger entry**; flag-OFF run ⇒ report bytes identical to baseline.
268	- **M7 anti-hardcode grep:** the diff introduces no literal `journal`/`peer_review`/`doi` outside the ONE T1-scholarly predicate (reused `_has_doi_or_journal_credential`) and no literal `review`/`Introduction and Scope` outside the archetype table. Then re-run M1–M6 asserting every behavioral delta traces to a contract field by diffing `SourceReceipt.basis` across swaps.
269	- **M8 invariant properties:** INV-3 verdict-monotonicity over all rows × all contracts; INV-1 via 0-diff on frozen files; adequacy boundary test (n = min_rows ± 1 flips degrade/enforce + disclosure line); Fix 4 body-line-multiset test (pre/post skeleton differ only by framing/boundary/preamble/order — verified sentences byte-identical).
270	- **M9 OFF-path golden:** champion PG_GATE-OFF render byte-identical with all flags default (covers both fixes).
271	
272	---
273	
274	## 7. OVER-ENGINEERING watch — what to NOT build
275	
276	- **No generative/LLM report skeleton;** no audience/tone/length layout logic; no per-archetype heading relabels (v1 keeps "## Key Findings" verbatim everywhere).
277	- **No credibility ontology subsystem.** The tier table is ≤4 predicates over already-fetched fields; if gov/news/PR host predicates aren't cheaply available, ship T1-scholarly + `kind∈allowed` only and TODO the rest — the *shape* is the deliverable.
278	- **Do NOT generalize `_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS`/quality tier bands** this run; the conflict ledger covers contradictory contracts.
279	- **Do NOT re-write the projection or the compiler** — `predicate_force` from term force is already wired (`retrieval_projection.py:674`); the projection already emits `op="prefer"` (:314).

# GATE — Generalize Fix 4 (report shape) + Fix 5 (source-kind/quality eligibility)

**Author:** OPUS (consolidation pass)
**Date:** 2026-07-17
**Baseline (source of truth):** `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent` @ `78fe2ca`
**Running build under review:** the working tree of `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent` (plan-faithful, journal-flavored Fix 4/5 — see §5 "what to unwind")

---

## 0. Which external models actually returned

- **FABLE 5 — RETURNED.** Full, code-anchored design (`fable.md`, 396 lines). This plan is built on it.
- **CODEX — FAILED.** `codex.md` is 0 bytes; `codex.err` contains only the echoed prompt/pack context, no model output. Codex contributed nothing; I do not attribute any view to it.
- **KIMI — FAILED.** No `kimi.md` was ever written (only `run_kimi.py` remains). Kimi contributed nothing.

So this is effectively **one external design (Fable) adjudicated against the real code by OPUS.** Where I write "the three models disagreed" below, the honest situation is: Fable proposed X, and OPUS's code cross-check disagreed with Fable on several concrete points (§4). There was no second/third model to triangulate.

### Path corrections applied to Fable's anchors (Fable inherited the pack's wrong paths)
- Quality code is at **`src/polaris_graph/retrieval/quality_eligibility.py`**, NOT `generator/quality_eligibility.py`. Line numbers (:177 score fn, :226 UNKNOWN return, :234 build fn, :142 predatory tuple) are correct.
- Projection code is at **`src/polaris_graph/planning/retrieval_projection.py`**, NOT `retrieval/`. `to_scope_protocol` at :262, the `allowed_source_kinds` prefer-facet loop at :314–:316, `is_empty` at :230.
- `key_findings.py` is at `src/polaris_graph/generator/key_findings.py` (correct). Bullet emit :924–:925, preamble :937, `refilter_key_findings_block` :815, `_MAX_BULLETS=6` :65.
- `is_peer_reviewed_journal_article` is in `src/polaris_graph/retrieval/document_type_classifier.py:448` and **takes a `DocumentType`, not a row** — you must `classify_document_type(...)` first. (The running build already does this correctly.)

---

## 1. Governing constraints (unchanged, restated)

- **C1 FAITHFULNESS FROZEN.** `provenance_generator.py`, strict_verify, drop rule, NLI, D8: **0-diff**. No new verification pass, no new faithfulness test.
- **C2 SCOPE AT RETRIEVAL, never filter a frozen corpus** (997→131 tanked RACE 0.4447→0.3264). Hard strength buys *go-find + reorder + audit*, not a mask — unless corpus is provably adequate.
- **C3 MINIMAL / anti-over-engineering.** Smallest faithfulness-safe change. This run's job is *prove-not-a-regression*, not beat-champion. One env flag per fix, default OFF/prior; champion path double-guarded.
- **C4 Coverage > Insight > Readability > IF** in the reward, but IF is the measured deficit we are closing.

---

## 2. FIX 4 — contract-driven report skeleton (generalize the report shape)

### 2.1 General data model: **closed archetype table, NOT generative**

**Decision: a small static archetype table keyed off normalized `deliverable.kind`.** I adopt Fable's Option 1 and its reasoning, and I make it *even smaller*.

Why table over generative:
- Generative (LLM designs the section order at render time) is a new subsystem, nondeterministic (un-metamorphic-testable), adds an LLM call on the render path, and the only way it "beats" a table is by reflowing text — which walks straight at C1. It also cannot be proven OFF-path byte-identical without a shadow render. Rejected under C3.
- The table is deterministic, ~1 file of pure data + 3 pure functions, trivially OFF-path byte-identical, and every behavioral delta traces to one contract field.

**The data model — the key simplification is that machinery placement is archetype-INVARIANT.** Machinery = {methods, `_cwf_disclosed_md`, `_drop_disclosure_md`, reliability, contradiction/reliability ledgers} goes below the *existing* appendix boundary `_AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY` (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:6325`) for **every** archetype. What varies per archetype is only **two** degrees of freedom:

```python
# NEW: src/polaris_graph/generator/report_skeleton.py  (pure; no I/O, no LLM)
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Archetype:
    key: str            # review | memo | brief | comparison | explainer
    framing_title: str  # "Introduction and Scope" | "" | "Executive Summary" | "Scope and Criteria" | "Overview"
    kf_position: str    # "lead" (BLUF) | "after_framing" | "tail"

ARCHETYPES = {
  "review":     Archetype("review",     "Introduction and Scope", "after_framing"),
  "memo":       Archetype("memo",       "",                       "lead"),
  "brief":      Archetype("brief",      "Executive Summary",      "lead"),
  "comparison": Archetype("comparison", "Scope and Criteria",     "after_framing"),
  "explainer":  Archetype("explainer",  "Overview",               "tail"),
}
KIND_SYNONYMS = {  # normalized deliverable.kind value -> archetype key
  "literature review":"review","systematic review":"review","survey":"review","review":"review",
  "memo":"memo","decision memo":"memo","briefing memo":"memo",
  "brief":"brief","policy brief":"brief","executive brief":"brief",
  "comparison":"comparison","comparative analysis":"comparison","market scan":"comparison",
  "explainer":"explainer","primer":"explainer","overview":"explainer",
}
DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE = "review"   # least-wrong universal shape; disclosed when assumed
```

- **`resolve_archetype(contract) -> (Archetype, assumed: bool)`** — reads `contract.deliverable` terms with `dimension == "deliverable.kind"`, lowercases the value, maps via `KIND_SYNONYMS`; unmatched/empty ⇒ `DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE` with `assumed=True`, and the caller appends an `Assumption` to `contract.assumptions` (the disclosure ledger, `planning_gate_schema.py:161`). A HARD *opaque* deliverable clause stays lossless per the NORM_OPAQUE rule — never silently coerced.
- **`build_framing_md(contract, archetype) -> str`** — a claim-free, citation-free, numeral-free framing paragraph built ONLY from `contract.objective` term values (the user's own prompt spans). Same faithfulness class as the already-shipped abort-path H1s (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:5862 / :5898`). Returns `""` when `framing_title == ""` (memo) or objective is empty.
- **`order_report_blocks(archetype, blocks: dict[str,str]) -> str`** — a **pure permutation + concat of opaque block strings.** Never re-flows a block interior.

Ordered skeleton over the blocks that already exist at `run_honest_sweep_r3.py` `_assembled_body`:

```
# {question}                        (H1 — the existing "# Research report: {q}", :17254)
[KF]           if kf_position == "lead"          (memo / brief — BLUF)
[framing]      if framing_title != ""            (## {framing_title} from objective)
[KF]           if kf_position == "after_framing" (review / comparison)
sections_concat                                  (thematic ### — NEVER reordered internally)
_depth_layer                                     (synthesis)
[KF]           if kf_position == "tail"          (explainer — keep heading "## Key Findings" v1)
biblio_section
## Appendix: audit, disclosure, and weighting (not scored as report claims)   (:6325 boundary, verbatim)
methods + _cwf_disclosed_md + _drop_disclosure_md          (machinery, verbatim, nothing deleted)
[reliability_md appended UNDER the same boundary by compose_report_with_reliability]
```

### 2.2 EXACT edits (Fix 4) — verified against the code

1. **NEW `src/polaris_graph/generator/report_skeleton.py`** — the three pure functions + tables above. No import of `provenance_generator`. Pure data (no network/LLM/I/O).

2. **`scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` `_assembled_body` seam (:17254 region)** — behind `PG_REPORT_SKELETON` (default **OFF**) **AND** a pinned `ResearchContract` present: replace the fixed concat with `order_report_blocks(archetype, blocks)`. Flag OFF **or** no contract ⇒ the *exact existing concat expression runs verbatim* (same "OFF ⇒ byte-identical" pattern as `assemble_report_md`'s no-summary branch :5230 and `compose_report_with_reliability`'s legacy prepend). The framing text and archetype heading are the only new lines on this path.

3. **`scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py` `compose_report_with_reliability` (:6313)** — one guarded addition: if the body **already contains** `_AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY` (only possible when the skeleton emitted it), append `rel` under the existing boundary instead of emitting a second boundary. Skeleton OFF ⇒ boundary absent from body ⇒ function byte-identical.

4. **`src/polaris_graph/generator/key_findings.py` bullet integrity (Fix 4c)** — **KEEP the running build's version as-is** (`_bullet_marker_integrity_ok` + `_reemit_key_findings_bullet`). It is already kind-agnostic; a malformed bullet is a bug in every archetype. No generalization needed. (See §5: no change to unwind here.)

5. **`key_findings.py:937` preamble (Fix 4d)** — **KEEP the running build's shrink as-is.** Already kind-agnostic. Gate it behind the same `PG_REPORT_SKELETON`? No — it is a pure quality edit with its own kill-switch already; keep the running build's guarding. No unwind.

6. **DO NOT touch the D8 banner prepend (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py:20778`).** The builder is frozen (`provenance_generator.py:3212`); the plan rejects `PG_REPORT_D8_BANNER=0`. ### 2.3 Faithfulness-safety (Fix 4)

- **`order_report_blocks` permutes at whole-block granularity.** Block interiors — every strict_verify-passed sentence in `sections_concat`, every KF bullet — are never re-worded, re-flowed, or re-cited. **Verified sentences are byte-identical** (checkable by a body-line-multiset test: pre/post differ only by order + the new framing/boundary lines).

- **Frozen files 0-diff:** no edit to / import from `provenance_generator.py`. Placement/repetition is QUALITY not faithfulness (CLAUDE.md §-1.3).
- **Upstream/OFF-path:** the seam is render assembly, downstream of strict_verify (verified text already fixed) and never touches the verifier.

### 2.4 PG_GATE-OFF byte-identity (Fix 4)

Two independent guards, both required to activate:
1. `PG_REPORT_SKELETON` default OFF (kill-switch defaults to prior behaviour).
2. Skeleton branches only when a pinned `ResearchContract` exists; the champion path passes `plan=None`/no contract, so a stray flag cannot fire.

When inactive, `:17254` runs the *original concat expression verbatim* (not a re-derived equivalent), `compose_report_with_reliability` takes its existing branch. **Test: golden-file byte-compare of a champion-path render before/after the patch.**

---

## 3. FIX 5 — contract-keyed source-kind + credibility-tier eligibility (generalize)

### 3.1 The projection is ALREADY general — do not add a branch

`RetrievalPolicy.to_scope_protocol` (`retrieval_projection.py:314–316`) already emits **every** `allowed_source_kinds` entry as `op="prefer"`, with `strictness="hard"` iff `predicate_force["allowed_source_kinds"]=="hard"`. **No journal literal there.** And the compile step is *already done*: `retrieval_projection.py:674` already sets `predicate_force["allowed_source_kinds"]="hard" if is_hard else "soft"` from the ContractTerm's `force` (which the clause ledger derives from the deontic cue family: restrict/oblige/exclude ⇒ FORCE_HARD; prefer/quality/date ⇒ FORCE_PREFER, `clause_ledger.py:240–261`).

**Adjudication (OPUS vs Fable):** Fable's edit B.(a).1 ("set `predicate_force['allowed_source_kinds']` from the term's force in the compiler") is **already in the baseline** — do NOT write it. This is the single most important code-check finding: the deontic-strength → policy wiring exists; Fix 5's generalization work is entirely in the three layers *around* the projection, not the projection or the compiler.

### 3.2 General credibility-TIER model for UNKNOWN resolution (journal ⇒ PASS is ONE instance)

Today `score_source_quality` (`quality_eligibility.py:177`) falls to UNKNOWN (fail-closed under hard) at `:222/:226` whenever tier metadata is absent (~41% of the corpus). The running build's rescue `_has_doi_or_journal_credential` recognizes **only DOI + peer-reviewed-journal** — journal-literal.

**General model — a deterministic signal→(tier, kind) table over fields the pipeline already fetches** (no new subsystem; ~30 LOC in `quality_eligibility.py`):

```python
# Tiers: T1 = authoritative-universal ; T2 = reputable-secondary ; T3 = interested-primary.
# (Low tiers T5-T7 already FAIL via _LOW_QUALITY_TIERS — untouched.)
def _positive_signal_tier(row, url) -> tuple[str, str] | None:   # -> (tier, kind) or None
    # scholarly: reuse the EXISTING running-build predicate verbatim (DOI or peer-reviewed journal)
    ok, _basis = _has_doi_or_journal_credential(row, url)
    if ok:
        return ("T1", "peer_reviewed_journal")     # <-- journal ⇒ PASS is JUST THIS ROW
    if _official_gov_host(url):                      # registry TLD / gov facet from the facet classifier
        return ("T1", "government")
    if _reputable_newsroom(row, url):                # wire/major-outlet facet, tier not low
        return ("T2", "news")
    if _issuer_primary(row, url):                    # press release / corp page on the issuer domain
        return ("T3", "press_release" if _is_pr(row) else "corporate")
    return None

# Resolution rule (inserted in score_source_quality AFTER step 4 (:214), BEFORE the :222 shell check):
#   sig = _positive_signal_tier(row, url)
#   if sig:
#       tier_id, kind = sig
#       if tier_id == "T1":                       PASS  (quality is quality on ANY contract)
#       elif kind in allowed_kinds and kind not in excluded_kinds:  PASS  (contract wants this kind)
#   # otherwise fall through to the existing UNKNOWN + demote weight
```

"journal ⇒ PASS" **falls out** as the T1-scholarly row. A gov-report contract gets `.gov` sources PASSing via T1-government unconditionally. A decision-memo contract with `allowed_source_kinds=["news","press_release"]` gets wire stories + issuer PRs PASSing via the T2/T3-∈-allowed clause — which no journal-literal implementation can produce.

**Over-engineering guard (OPUS):** keep the tier table to the **4 predicates the running build's helpers already imply** — do NOT build a credibility ontology. If `_official_gov_host` / `_reputable_newsroom` / `_issuer_primary` cannot be sourced cheaply from the existing facet classifier, ship **only the T1-scholarly + the "kind ∈ allowed" gate seeded by the existing journal helper** for v1, and leave T2/T3 host predicates as a labelled TODO. The generalization that matters most is the *shape* (signal→tier→∈allowed), so that adding a kind later is data, not a new branch.

### 3.3 EXACT edits (Fix 5) — verified against the code

1. **`quality_eligibility.py:score_source_quality` (:177)** — add keyword-only params `allowed_kinds: frozenset[str] = frozenset()`, `excluded_kinds: frozenset[str] = frozenset()` (defaults empty ⇒ every existing caller byte-identical). Replace the running build's journal-only second-chance (`_has_doi_or_journal_credential` PASS at "4.5") with the `_positive_signal_tier` resolution rule, inserted **after step 4 (:214, the low-tier FAIL) and before step 5 (:216, the shell UNKNOWN)** — so every FAIL return (retracted :193, predatory :199, `is_peer_reviewed=False` :207, low-tier :214) is lexically above it and untouched. `_has_doi_or_journal_credential` is **reused** as the T1-scholarly predicate (single source of truth, no duplicate journal logic).

2. **`quality_eligibility.py:build_quality_eligibility` (:234)** — thread `allowed_kinds=frozenset(policy.allowed_source_kinds)`, `excluded_kinds=frozenset(policy.excluded_source_kinds)` into the `score_source_quality` call. Receipts carry the new basis strings automatically.

3. **`quality_eligibility.py:_PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS` (:142)** — **KEEP the running build's `"abacademies.org"` entry.** Kind-agnostic denylist; no change.

4. **`PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING=1` (Fix 5a)** — enrichment only, feeds the existing `tier` field read at :201. Already kind-agnostic; keep.

5. **Topicality two-tier (Fix 5c) at `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:14369` region** — **KEEP the running build's `PG_TOPICALITY_HARD_FLOOR=0.15` + soft-demote of the 0.15–0.30 band as-is.** Already fully kind-agnostic (embedding cosine vs objective). No change.

6. **Selection ordering (Fix 5d) — GENERALIZE the running build.** The running build sorts on `_is_journal_lead` (`"journal" in str(_k).lower()` + `_jo_credential`). Replace with a **kind-match key**:
```python
_allowed = frozenset(str(k).lower() for k in (getattr(_gate_policy,"allowed_source_kinds",None) or []))
def _kind_match(_r) -> bool:
    return classified_kind(_r) in _allowed      # classified_kind from the SAME facet classifier
evidence_for_gen = sorted(evidence_for_gen, key=lambda _r: 0 if _kind_match(_r) else 1)  # stable
```
Preserve the running build's `assert len == _n_before` count-invariant and the log line (reword "journal" → "in-scope-kind"). When `_allowed` is empty ⇒ every row is `False` ⇒ stable no-op reorder (byte-identical selection).

### 3.4 Invariants and how each is coded

- **INV-1 never re-admit a strict_verify FAIL.** Structural: all Fix-5 code lives in *eligibility* (which rows may enter the citable menu), always upstream of the frozen verifier. Eligibility neither reads nor writes verify verdicts; a PASS-promoted row's claims still go through the untouched verifier. Enforced by C1's 0-diff check.
- **INV-2 never override a contract EXCLUSION.** Two layers: (i) in the tier rule, `kind in excluded_kinds` is never PASSed (the T1 clause is unconditional PASS, so a T1-host *excluded-kind* row — e.g. a `.gov`-hosted **blog** under exclude-blogs — must be handled: **T1 PASS is by tier, but the exclude facet masks it out of the menu before selection**, so it never cites; (ii) exclude facet at `retrieval_projection.py` (`op="exclude"`, hard iff NOT_IN term FORCE_HARD) operates regardless of quality verdict. **OPUS refinement of Fable:** Fable claimed the tier rule itself blocks excluded kinds; but the T1 clause is unconditional in the table. The real guarantee is the exclude facet mask upstream of the menu — so state that as the load-bearing INV-2 layer, not the tier rule. Test: a T1 `.gov`-hosted blog under exclude-blogs must not appear in the citable menu/bibliography.
- **INV-3 evidence-POSITIVE only (verdict monotonicity).** The inserted block contains ONLY `return PASS`; every FAIL return is lexically above and untouched; UNKNOWN fall-throughs remain the else-path. Property: `∀ row, contract: new_verdict ∈ {old_verdict} ∪ ({PASS} if old_verdict==UNKNOWN else {})`. The kind-match ordering assigns no weights/verdicts, so it cannot newly fail anything.

### 3.5 Corpus-adequacy + disclosure fallback (ANY kind, not just journals)

```python
# quality_eligibility.py (new, ~15 LOC — NOT a new module)
def corpus_kind_adequacy(rows, allowed_kinds, *, min_rows=25) -> tuple[bool, int]:
    n = sum(1 for r in rows
            if classified_kind(r) in allowed_kinds
            and score_source_quality(r, allowed_kinds=allowed_kinds)[0] != FAIL)
    return (n >= min_rows, n)
```

At the sweep call-site the kind-restriction `is_hard` arms **only** as `term_is_hard AND env_flag(PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD) AND adequate`; otherwise force degrades to *prefer* and one disclosure line is appended to the machinery block (which Fix 4 places in the appendix):

> "Scope note: the prompt restricts sources to {kinds}; only {n} in-scope sources were retrievable, below the {min_rows} adequacy floor — in-scope sources were prioritized rather than exclusively enforced."

This generalizes 5d(3): the plan's "≥25 T1/T2/DOI rows" measures *scholarliness*; the general count measures *in-scope-kind-ness*. On a 200-wire-story news-memo corpus the journal-literal count would flunk adequacy (or arm on the wrong thing) while the general count correctly passes. Adequacy gates only the *eligibility hard mask*; retrieval-side "go find in-scope sources" (C2) always runs — that keeps the count honest, not a self-fulfilling starvation.

**Flag rename:** `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY` → `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD` (kind-agnostic; reads kinds from the policy). The journal flag has **not shipped** (running build never added it — only selection ordering) so rename with **no alias** (C3). Default OFF.

**Latent journal-centrism — FLAGGED, NOT fixed (C3), with an OPUS code correction:** Fable claims `_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS` "FAILs `tier ∈ {industry/news/blog/stub}` — kind words inside a quality table." **Code check: this is imprecise.** `_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS = {"T5","T6","T7"}` (`quality_eligibility.py:137`) keys on **tier IDs, not kind words**; "industry/news/blog/stub" appears only in the human-readable *basis string* at :214. So there is **no kind-literal in the mask to generalize away** — the tier→quality mapping is upstream (whatever assigns T5-T7). The real contradiction case ("high-quality only" + "cite blogs") is a genuine user contradiction that must surface in `ResearchContract.conflicts` (`planning_gate_schema.py:162`), not be silently resolved. And `build_quality_eligibility` only arms when the contract explicitly requests high quality (`_is_high_quality_request` gate at :... tokens :... ), so a blogs-wanted contract without a quality clause never enters this gate. **V1 leaves the tier band untouched; do NOT generalize the tier table this run.**

### 3.6 Faithfulness-safety (Fix 5)

- All Fix-5 changes shape the citable **menu** upstream of the frozen verifier; no FAIL row re-admitted (INV-3); UNKNOWN handling adds *evidence* (positive signal), not leniency; `provenance_generator.py` / strict_verify **0-diff**.
- Selection ordering is promote-by-sort only — no weight, no verdict, count-invariant (`assert len == _n_before`).
- **PG_GATE-OFF byte-identical:** `allowed_kinds`/`excluded_kinds` default empty ⇒ `score_source_quality` byte-identical; `corpus_kind_adequacy` never called without a policy; the kind-match sort is a stable no-op when `_allowed` is empty; `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD` default OFF; `RetrievalPolicy.is_empty()` (:230) ⇒ caller applies NO filter. Champion path (no contract) ⇒ all Fix-5 code no-ops.

---

Only Fable returned, so "disagreement" = OPUS's code cross-check vs Fable's claims:

Shared fixture: one synthetic **60-row corpus** with known ground truth — 10 DOI journals, 12 `.gov` reports, 15 wire news, 8 issuer press releases, 8 analyst blogs, 7 metadata-less UNKNOWNs — plus contract fixtures. **Zero code differences between runs; the only variable is the swapped contract.**
